task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the required drawing layers with correct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel (usually on the right side of LibreCAD). Verify that layers named 'CONCRETE' (with a gray color icon), 'REBAR_MAIN' (with a red color icon), and 'REBAR_STIRRUPS' (with a green color icon) exist.

task_completion.description: Verify the concrete beam outline is drawn on the correct layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing area. There should be a large rectangular outline drawn in gray (representing the 5000x600 beam).

task_completion.description: Verify the top and bottom longitudinal reinforcement bars are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the gray beam outline. There should be two long horizontal red lines, one near the top and one near the bottom, representing the main rebar.

task_completion.description: Verify the vertical stirrups are drawn and spaced along the beam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look along the length of the beam. There should be a series of evenly spaced vertical green lines (approximately 25 of them) intersecting the red horizontal lines.

task_completion.description: Verify the drawing is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the LibreCAD window title bar or a 'Save As' dialog box. It should display the filename 'rc_beam_elevation.dxf', indicating the file has been saved.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD graphical user interface must be visible in the screenshots, showing the drawing canvas, toolbars, and layer panels being actively used.

integrity.description: Ensure the drawing was created manually rather than just opening a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD command line area (usually at the bottom) should show evidence of drawing commands being executed (e.g., 'line', 'rect', 'layer', 'offset').

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use external scripts or text editors to generate the DXF file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open running Python scripts (like ezdxf) or text editors manually writing DXF code to bypass using the LibreCAD GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a LibreCAD drawing saved as 'rc_beam_elevation.dxf'. The drawing must contain three specific layers: 'CONCRETE' (Color: Gray/8), 'REBAR_MAIN' (Color: Red/1), and 'REBAR_STIRRUPS' (Color: Green/3). The beam outline should be 5000x600 units (gray). Main reinforcement consists of two horizontal lines (red) at Y=40 and Y=560. Stirrups are vertical lines (green) starting at X=100 and spaced every 200 units (approx 25 stirrups total).
